calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Amended Petition Of Writ Of Mandate *****Filed At Insistence Of Litigant*****
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Earnestine May-McNary, plaintiff in pro per, appearing on her own behalf, by telephone;
Rebecca Liu, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3870, for defendant CCSF Retirement Board.

RULING: For the benefit of petitioner in pro per who did not have computer access, the Court read the gist of the tentative ruling. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PETITIONER EARNESTINE MAY-MCNARY's Motion For Amended Petition Of Writ Of Mandate, as follows:

Petitioner Earnestine May-McNary's petition for writ of mandate is denied.

The court employs the independent judgment rule when analyzing petitioner's writ of mandate because she has a fundamental vested right to the disability benefits if in fact she was disabled in 2004 when she stopped working for respondent. (See Beckley v. Board of Administration (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 691, 697.) "[I]n exercising its independent judgment, a trial court must afford a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings, and the party challenging the administrative decision bears the burden of convincing the court that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence." (Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 817.)

SF Charter A8.587-3 provides in pertinent part that "[a]ny member who becomes
incapacitated for performance of duty because of disability determined by a qualified
hearing officer to be of extended and uncertain duration" is entitled to disability benefits.
"[T]o be 'incapacitated for the performance of duty'?means the substantial inability of the
applicant to perform his usual duties." (Mansperger v. Public Employees Retirement System
(1970) 6 Cal.App.3d 873, 876.)

In this case, petitioner fails to show that Administrative Law Judge Owyang's decision to deny petitioner's second application for disability benefits is contrary to the weight of the evidence. The record demonstrates that no physician advised petitioner to stop working in 2004 and she was first told to do so in 2010. (AR1 208:24-25; 209:1-25; 210:1-6.) The medical evidence supports the finding that petitioner was not incapacitated for the performance of her duty as a medical transcriber in 2004. (AR2 124-138 (Dr. Talmage's report, pg. 14); AR2 87-110 (Dr. Perl's report, pgs. 23-24.) Lastly, and most importantly, petitioner admitted that she worked in her customary position as a medical transcriber for Sutter Health from 2008-2010. (AR2 280:10-24; 282:14-16.) This evidence strongly suggests that petitioner could perform her regular duties in 2004. Petitioner's credibility was also lacking because when she first applied for benefits, she denied taking any post-retirement employment. (AR1 192:6-11; 211:9-11.) The administrative law judge noted this omission and could reasonably conclude that all or some of petitioner's testimony lacked credibility.

Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org

First Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Petitioner EARNESTINE MAY-MCNARY (Tel: 415-822-7466) appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER EARNESTINE MAY-MCNARY's First Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate.

The Court notes that after the court's issuance of its tentative ruling in this matter, petitioner did not inform the opposing side of her intent to appear today to contest that tentative ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER EARNESTINE MAY-MCNARY'S First Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate is off calendar. As noted by a letter from respondent, petitioner failed to provide the required 16 court days of notice for hearing in compliance with Code of Civ. Proc., 1005.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
